
```kotlin
val foo = { /* no-op */ }
```

```kotlin
val foo = { 
    /* no-op */ 
}
```

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.BLOCK_COMMENT
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.EOL_COMMENT
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHITE_SPACE
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.hasNewLineInClosedRange
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.indent
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 15→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 16→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 17→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiCommentImpl
 18→
 19→/**
 20→ * Checks external wrapping of block comments. Wrapping inside the comment is not altered. A block comment following another element on the
 21→ * same line is replaced with an EOL comment, if possible.
 22→ */
 23→public class CommentWrappingRule :
 24→    StandardRule(
 25→        id = "comment-wrapping",
 26→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 27→            setOf(
 28→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 29→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 30→            ),
 31→    ) {
 32→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 33→        node: ASTNode,
 34→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 35→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 36→    ) {
 37→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 38→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment =
 39→                node
 40→                    .prevLeaf()
 41→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 42→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment =
 43→                node
 44→                    .nextLeaf()
 45→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 46→
 47→            if (nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment != null &&
 48→                nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment != null
 49→            ) {
 50→                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment, nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment)) {
 51→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 52→                    //    val foo = "foo" /*
 53→                    //    some comment
 54→                    //    */ val bar = "bar"
 55→                    emit(
 56→                        node.startOffset,
 57→                        "A block comment starting on same line as another element and ending on another line before another element is " +
 58→                            "disallowed",
 59→                        false,
 60→                    )
 61→                } else {
 62→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 63→                    //    val foo /* some comment */ = "foo"
 64→                    emit(
 65→                        node.startOffset,
 66→                        "A block comment in between other elements on the same line is disallowed",
 67→                        false,
 68→                    )
 69→                }
 70→                return
 71→            }
 72→
 73→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment
 74→                ?.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 75→
 76→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment
 77→                ?.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 78→        }
 79→    }
 80→
 81→    private fun ASTNode.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(
 82→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
 83→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 84→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 85→    ) {
 86→        val leafAfterBlockComment = blockCommentNode.nextLeaf()
 87→        if (!blockCommentNode.textContains('\n') && leafAfterBlockComment.isLastElementOnLine()) {
 88→            emit(
 89→                startOffset,
 90→                "A single line block comment after a code element on the same line must be replaced with an EOL comment",
 91→                true,
 92→            )
 93→            if (autoCorrect) {
 94→                blockCommentNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
 95→            }
 96→        } else {
 97→            // It can not be autocorrected as it might depend on the situation and code style what is preferred.
 98→            emit(
 99→                blockCommentNode.startOffset,
100→                "A block comment after any other element on the same line must be separated by a new line",
101→                false,
102→            )
103→        }
104→    }
105→
106→    private fun ASTNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment() {
107→        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()
108→        val eolComment = PsiCommentImpl(EOL_COMMENT, "// $content")
109→        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)
110→        rawRemove()
111→    }
112→
113→    private fun ASTNode.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(
114→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
115→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
116→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
117→    ) {
118→        emit(startOffset, "A block comment may not be followed by any other element on that same line", true)
119→        if (autoCorrect) {

 23→/**
 24→ * Checks external wrapping of block comments. Wrapping inside the comment is not altered. A block comment following another element on the
 25→ * same line is replaced with an EOL comment, if possible.
 26→ */
 27→public class CommentWrappingRule :
 28→    StandardRule(
 29→        id = "comment-wrapping",
 30→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 31→            setOf(
 32→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 33→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 34→            ),
 35→    ) {
 36→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 37→        node: ASTNode,
 38→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 39→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 40→    ) {
 41→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 42→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment =
 43→                node
 44→                    .prevLeaf()
 45→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 46→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment =
 47→                node
 48→                    .nextLeaf()
 49→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 50→
 51→            if (nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment != null &&
 52→                nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment != null
 53→            ) {
 54→                // Allow block comments inside single-line blocks: { /* no-op */ }
 55→                // Check if the block comment is between braces (skipping whitespace)
 56→                val prevCode = node.prevCodeLeaf()
 57→                val nextCode = node.nextCodeLeaf()
 58→                if (prevCode?.elementType == LBRACE &&
 59→                    nextCode?.elementType == RBRACE &&
 60→                    !hasNewLineInClosedRange(prevCode, nextCode)
 61→                ) {
 62→                    // This is a valid single-line block with a comment inside
 63→                    return
 64→                }
 65→
 66→                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment, nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment)) {
 67→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 68→                    //    val foo = "foo" /*
 69→                    //    some comment
 70→                    //    */ val bar = "bar"
 71→                    emit(
 72→                        node.startOffset,
 73→                        "A block comment starting on same line as another element and ending on another line before another element is " +
 74→                            "disallowed",
 75→                        false,
 76→                    )
 77→                } else {
 78→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 79→                    //    val foo /* some comment */ = "foo"
 80→                    emit(
 81→                        node.startOffset,
 82→                        "A block comment in between other elements on the same line is disallowed",
 83→                        false,
 84→                    )
 85→                }
 86→                return
 87→            }
 88→
 89→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment
 90→                ?.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 91→
 92→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment
 93→                ?.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 94→        }
 95→    }
 96→
 97→    private fun ASTNode.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(
 98→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
 99→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
100→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
101→    ) {
102→        val leafAfterBlockComment = blockCommentNode.nextLeaf()
103→        if (!blockCommentNode.textContains('\n') && leafAfterBlockComment.isLastElementOnLine()) {
104→            emit(
105→                startOffset,
106→                "A single line block comment after a code element on the same line must be replaced with an EOL comment",
107→                true,
108→            )
109→            if (autoCorrect) {
110→                blockCommentNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
111→            }
112→        } else {
113→            // It can not be autocorrected as it might depend on the situation and code style what is preferred.
114→            emit(
115→                blockCommentNode.startOffset,
116→                "A block comment after any other element on the same line must be separated by a new line",
117→                false,
118→            )
119→        }
120→    }
121→
122→    private fun ASTNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment() {
123→        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()
124→        val eolComment = PsiCommentImpl(EOL_COMMENT, "// $content")
125→        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)
126→        rawRemove()
127→    }
128→
129→    private fun ASTNode.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(
130→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
131→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
132→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
133→    ) {
134→        emit(startOffset, "A block comment may not be followed by any other element on that same line", true)
135→        if (autoCorrect) {
136→            this.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(blockCommentNode.indent())
137→        }

## Summary of Changes

The issue was that ktlint's `CommentWrappingRule` was incorrectly flagging block comments inside single-line blocks like `val foo = { /* no-op */ }` as violations. The rule was designed to prevent block comments between other elements on the same line, but it was too strict and didn't account for the valid use case of a block comment being the only content inside a lambda/block.

### Changes Made:

1. **Modified `CommentWrappingRule.kt`**:
   - Added imports for `LBRACE`, `RBRACE`, `prevCodeLeaf`, and `nextCodeLeaf`
   - Added logic to detect when a block comment is the only content inside a single-line block (between `{` and `}`)
   - Uses `prevCodeLeaf()` and `nextCodeLeaf()` to skip whitespace and check for the actual brace tokens
   - If the comment is between braces on a single line, it returns early without emitting an error

2. **Created `CommentWrappingRuleSingleLineBlockTest.kt`**:
   - New test file with comprehensive tests for the fix
   - Tests various scenarios: single-line blocks with comments, multiple blocks, blocks without spaces, etc.
   - Also tests that multiline comments inside blocks still raise errors as expected

The fix is minimal and targeted, only affecting the specific case where a block comment is the only content inside a single-line block. All existing tests pass, and the new tests verify the fix works correctly.

content.text:
## Summary

I have successfully implemented a fix for the issue where ktlint was incorrectly flagging block comments inside single-line blocks.

### Problem
The `CommentWrappingRule` was treating block comments between braces (like `val foo = { /* no-op */ }`) as violations, even though this is a valid and common pattern for empty lambda expressions or no-op blocks.

### Solution

**Modified `CommentWrappingRule.kt`:**
1. Added imports for `LBRACE`, `RBRACE`, `prevCodeLeaf()`, and `nextCodeLeaf()`
2. Added logic (lines 54-64) to detect when a block comment is the only content inside a single-line block
3. Uses `prevCodeLeaf()` and `nextCodeLeaf()` to skip whitespace and check if the comment is between `{` and `}`
4. If the pattern matches and there are no newlines in the range, the rule returns early without emitting an error

**Created `CommentWrappingRuleSingleLineBlockTest.kt`:**
- New test file with comprehensive test cases
- Tests single-line blocks with block comments (the main issue)
- Tests multiple scenarios including blocks without spaces
- Verifies that multiline comments still raise errors as expected

### Verification
- ✅ All existing tests pass (entire ktlint-ruleset-standard test suite)
- ✅ New tests pass and cover the fix
- ✅ Manual testing confirms `val foo = { /* no-op */ }` is now accepted without violations
- ✅ No regressions in other comment-related rules

The fix is minimal, targeted, and only affects the specific case of block comments being the sole content inside single-line blocks, exactly as requested in the issue.

 23→/**
 24→ * Checks external wrapping of block comments. Wrapping inside the comment is not altered. A block comment following another element on the
 25→ * same line is replaced with an EOL comment, if possible.
 26→ */
 27→public class CommentWrappingRule :
 28→    StandardRule(
 29→        id = "comment-wrapping",
 30→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 31→            setOf(
 32→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 33→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 34→            ),
 35→    ) {
 36→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 37→        node: ASTNode,
 38→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 39→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 40→    ) {
 41→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 42→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment =
 43→                node
 44→                    .prevLeaf()
 45→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 46→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment =
 47→                node
 48→                    .nextLeaf()
 49→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 50→
 51→            if (nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment != null &&
 52→                nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment != null
 53→            ) {
 54→                // Allow block comments inside single-line blocks: { /* no-op */ }
 55→                // Check if the block comment is between braces (skipping whitespace)
 56→                val prevCode = node.prevCodeLeaf()
 57→                val nextCode = node.nextCodeLeaf()
 58→                if (prevCode?.elementType == LBRACE &&
 59→                    nextCode?.elementType == RBRACE &&
 60→                    !hasNewLineInClosedRange(prevCode, nextCode)
 61→                ) {
 62→                    // This is a valid single-line block with a comment inside
 63→                    return
 64→                }
 65→
 66→                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment, nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment)) {
 67→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 68→                    //    val foo = "foo" /*
 69→                    //    some comment
 70→                    //    */ val bar = "bar"
 71→                    emit(
 72→                        node.startOffset,
 73→                        "A block comment starting on same line as another element and ending on another line before another element is " +
 74→                            "disallowed",
 75→                        false,
 76→                    )
 77→                } else {
 78→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 79→                    //    val foo /* some comment */ = "foo"
 80→                    emit(
 81→                        node.startOffset,
 82→                        "A block comment in between other elements on the same line is disallowed",
 83→                        false,
 84→                    )
 85→                }
 86→                return
 87→            }
 88→
 89→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment
 90→                ?.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 91→
 92→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment
 93→                ?.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 94→        }
 95→    }
 96→
 97→    private fun ASTNode.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(
 98→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
 99→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
100→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
101→    ) {
102→        val leafAfterBlockComment = blockCommentNode.nextLeaf()
103→        if (!blockCommentNode.textContains('\n') && leafAfterBlockComment.isLastElementOnLine()) {
104→            emit(
105→                startOffset,
106→                "A single line block comment after a code element on the same line must be replaced with an EOL comment",
107→                true,
108→            )
109→            if (autoCorrect) {
110→                blockCommentNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
111→            }
112→        } else {
113→            // It can not be autocorrected as it might depend on the situation and code style what is preferred.
114→            emit(
115→                blockCommentNode.startOffset,
116→                "A block comment after any other element on the same line must be separated by a new line",
117→                false,
118→            )
119→        }
120→    }
121→
122→    private fun ASTNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment() {
123→        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()
124→        val eolComment = PsiCommentImpl(EOL_COMMENT, "// $content")
125→        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)
126→        rawRemove()
127→    }
128→
129→    private fun ASTNode.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(
130→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
131→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
132→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
133→    ) {
134→        emit(startOffset, "A block comment may not be followed by any other element on that same line", true)
135→        if (autoCorrect) {
136→            this.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(blockCommentNode.indent())
137→        }
138→    }
139→
140→    private fun isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it: ASTNode) = it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !it.textContains('\n')
141→
142→    private fun ASTNode?.isLastElementOnLine() = this == null || (elementType == WHITE_SPACE && textContains('\n'))
143→}
144→
145→public val COMMENT_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = CommentWrappingRule().ruleId
